code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a celestial body into a single fluent sentence.
    The function assumes all triples refer to the same subject (the celestial body).
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    subject = triples[0].subject

    def article(word: str) -> str:
        return "an" if word[0].lower() in "aeiou" else "a"

    # Mapping of predicates to more natural phrasing
    # Helper to clean object strings (remove quotes, parentheses, extra spaces)
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip()
        # Remove surrounding double quotes if present
        if o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"'):
            o = o[1:-1]
        # Replace parenthesised units with a space
        if " (" in o and o.endswith(")"):
            o = o.replace(" (", " ").rstrip(")")
        return o

    # Mapping predicates to readable labels
    label_map = {
        "apoapsis": "apoapsis",
        "periapsis": "periapsis",
        "epoch": "epoch",
        "orbitalPeriod": "orbital period",
        "averageSpeed": "average speed",
        "escapeVelocity": "escape velocity",
        "mass": "mass",
        "temperature": "temperature",
        "density": "density",
        "rotationPeriod": "rotation period",
        "maximumTemperature": "maximum temperature",
        "meanTemperature": "mean temperature",
        "minimumTemperature": "minimum temperature",
        "formerName": "former name",
        "absoluteMagnitude": "absolute magnitude",
        "discovered": "discovery date",
        "surfaceArea": "surface area",
        "deathCause": "cause of death",
        "deathPlace": "place of death",
        "deathDate": "date of death",
        "birthDate": "date of birth",
        "birthPlace": "place of birth",
        "nationality": "nationality",
        "stateOfOrigin": "state of origin",
        "almaMater": "alma mater",
        "doctoralStudent": "doctoral student",
    }

    def phrase_for(triple: Triple) -> str:
        p = triple.predicate
        o = clean_obj(triple.object)

        # Verb‑phrase predicates
        if p == "discoverer":
            return f"was discovered by {o}"
        if p == "discovered":
            return f"was discovered on {o}"
        if p == "deathDate":
            return f"died on {o}"
        if p == "birthDate":
            return f"was born on {o}"
        if p == "deathPlace":
            return f"died in {o}"
        if p == "birthPlace":
            return f"was born in {o}"
        if p == "deathCause":
            return f"died from {o}"
        if p in ("nationality", "stateOfOrigin"):
            return f"is {article(o)} {o}"

        # Default noun‑phrase construction using readable label
        label = label_map.get(p, p)
        return f"{article(label)} {label} of {o}"

    # Build phrases for each triple
    phrases = [phrase_for(t) for t in triples]

    # Combine phrases into a single sentence
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        body = phrases[0]
    else:
        body = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]

    # If the first phrase already contains a verb (“was discovered …”), drop the leading “has”
    if phrases[0].startswith("was "):
        return f"{subject} {body}."
    else:
        return f"{subject} has {body}."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
